Class RoleService

java.lang.Object
de.hallerweb.enterprise.prioritize.service.security.RoleService
@Service @Transactional public class RoleService extends Object
CRUD service for Roles, including assignment to a Department and management of a role's owned PermissionRecords (the backing operations for the admin C/R/U/D permission matrix). A role's permissions are a cascade-all / orphan-removal collection, so adding/removing a record through this service persists or deletes it along with the role.

Like UserService, this is an admin-console service and therefore carries no per-call authorization parameters — access is gated by the admin login in front of the UI.

  • Method Details

    • getAllRoles

      @Transactional(readOnly=true) public List<Role> getAllRoles()

    • getRoleById

      @Transactional(readOnly=true) public Role getRoleById(Long id)

    • getRoleByName

      @Transactional(readOnly=true) public Role getRoleByName(String name)

    • searchRoles

      @Transactional(readOnly=true) public List<Role> searchRoles(String phrase)

    • getRolesByDepartment

      @Transactional(readOnly=true) public List<Role> getRolesByDepartment(Long departmentId)

    • createRole

      public Role createRole(Role role, Long departmentId)
      Persists a new role. If departmentId is non-null the role is scoped to that department; null leaves it unscoped. The permission collection is initialized so the role is immediately ready for the permission matrix.

    • updateRole

      public Role updateRole(Long id, Role details, Long departmentId)
      Updates a role's base fields (name, description) and its department assignment. The permission collection and user assignments are left untouched here — use addPermissionToRole(Long, PermissionRecord) / removePermissionFromRole(Long, Long) for the matrix.

    • deleteRole

      public void deleteRole(Long id)
      Deletes a role. The role is first detached from every user that holds it (the user_roles join table is owned by PUser, so the association must be cleared from the owning side before the role row can be removed), then deleted along with its owned permission records. The holders are queried authoritatively from the join table rather than via the role's inverse users collection, which may be stale or unloaded in the current session.

    • addPermissionToRole

      public Role addPermissionToRole(Long roleId, PermissionRecord permission)
      Adds a permission record to a role. Thanks to cascade-all the record is persisted together with the role.

    • removePermissionFromRole

      public Role removePermissionFromRole(Long roleId, Long permissionId)
      Removes a permission record from a role. Thanks to orphan removal the detached record is deleted from the database.
